ext.description: Browse, search, tag, import/export, and manage local Codex CLI / Claude Code session history.
viewContainer.title: Codex History
cfg.title: Codex History Viewer
cfg.sessionsRoot: Root folder of Codex sessions. Leave empty to use the default (~/.codex/sessions).
cfg.claudeSessionsRoot: Root folder of Claude Code sessions. Leave empty to use the default (~/.claude/projects).
cfg.sourcesEnabled: Enable history sources to load. Choose one or both of: codex, claude.
cfg.previewOpenOnSelection: Open a reusable session preview in the editor area when selecting a tree item.
cfg.previewMaxMessages: Max number of user/assistant messages to include in detailed tree item tooltips.
cfg.previewTooltipMode: Choose how much information session tree item tooltips show.
cfg.previewTooltipMode.enumFull: Show date, source, CWD, tags/note, message excerpts, and action help.
cfg.previewTooltipMode.enumCompact: Show metadata only, without message excerpts or action help.
cfg.previewTooltipMode.enumTitleOnly: Show only the same one-line text shown in the tree item.
cfg.searchMaxResults: Max number of search hits to collect.
cfg.searchIndexToolContent: Choose how much tool content is stored in the search index. Including tool content can help File AI Change History prioritize related sessions.
cfg.searchIndexToolContent.enumConversat: Search only user, assistant, and developer messages. File AI Change History still works, but related-session prioritization may be less precise because file-name hints are limited.
cfg.searchIndexToolContent.enumToolCalls: Search messages plus tool names and arguments, without tool output.
Search messages, tool names/arguments, and tool output.
cfg.deleteUseTrash: When deleting, move files to the OS trash/recycle bin (recommended).
cfg.resumeOpenTarget: How 'Resume in OpenAI Codex' opens the conversation (sidebar or panel). Default is sidebar.
cfg.resumeOpenTarget.enumSidebar: Resume in the OpenAI Codex sidebar (recommended default).
cfg.resumeOpenTarget.enumPanel: Resume in an OpenAI Codex panel tab.
cfg.uiLanguage: Choose the display language used by this extension. Auto follows VS Code's display language.
cfg.uiLanguage.enumJapanese: Always show this extension in Japanese.
cfg.uiLanguage.enumEnglish: Always show this extension in English.
cfg.uiTimeGuideEnabled: Show the date guide and bookmark controls in history views.
cfg.uiAlwaysShowHeaderActions: Always show view header action icons (sets VS Code setting 'workbench.view.alwaysShowHeaderActions').
cfg.historyDateBasis: Choose which session date is used by the history tree and date-based search filters.
cfg.historyDateBasis.enumStarted: Group history and apply date filters using the session start date.
cfg.historyDateBasis.enumLastActivity: Group history and apply date filters using the latest session activity date.
cfg.historyTitleSource: Choose how session titles are displayed.
cfg.historyTitleSource.enumGenerated: Create titles from the conversation content.
cfg.historyTitleSource.enumNativeWhenAva: Prefer title metadata from Codex or Claude when available, and otherwise create titles from the conversation content.
cfg.fileChangeHistoryExplorerContextMenu: Show 'File AI Change History' in the Explorer file context menu.
cfg.autoRefreshEnabled: Automatically refresh history when local session files change. Disabled by default to avoid background watchers unless you opt in.
cfg.autoRefreshDebounceMs: Delay before auto-refreshing after session file changes. Multiple file events during this window are merged.
cfg.autoRefreshMinIntervalMs: Minimum interval between automatic history refreshes.
cfg.chatOpenPosition: Choose where a chat session opens when switching back to a previously viewed session.
cfg.chatOpenPosition.enumTop: Open from the top when no target message is specified.
cfg.chatOpenPosition.enumLastMessage: Restore near the last viewed message.
cfg.chatOpenPosition.enumLatest: Open at the latest rendered card.
cfg.chatPerformanceMode: Controls the default performance mode for history views. Auto switches based on history size and diff count. Normal preserves the view state as much as possible, but large conversations may take longer to load or switch back to. Simplified loads diffs and details on demand, and heavy rendered sections are lightened when the view is restored.
cfg.chatPerformanceMode.enumAuto: Automatically choose Normal or Simplified based on history size and diff count.
cfg.chatPerformanceMode.enumNormal: Preserve the view state as much as possible. Large conversations may take longer to load or switch back to.
cfg.chatPerformanceMode.enumSimplified: Load diffs and details on demand, and lighten heavy rendered sections when the view is restored.
cfg.chatToolDisplayMode: Choose how tool activity is shown in the chat view.
cfg.chatToolDisplayMode.enumDetailsOnly: Keep the current behavior. Tool items appear only when details are shown.
cfg.chatToolDisplayMode.enumCompactCards: Always show tool cards, while 'Show details' only controls raw tool details and notes.
cfg.chatUserLongMessageFolding: Choose how long user messages are collapsed in the chat view.
cfg.chatUserLongMessageFolding.enumOff: Keep the current behavior. User messages are always fully expanded.
cfg.chatUserLongMessageFolding.enumAuto: Collapse only long user messages when details are hidden.
cfg.chatUserLongMessageFolding.enumAlway: Always start eligible user messages collapsed when details are hidden.
cfg.chatAssistantLongMessageFolding: Choose how long assistant messages are collapsed in the chat view.
cfg.chatAssistantLongMessageFolding.enum: Keep the current behavior. Assistant messages are always fully expanded.
Collapse only long assistant messages when details are hidden.
Always start eligible assistant messages collapsed when details are hidden.
cfg.imagesEnabled: Show supported image attachments in the chat view.
cfg.imagesMaxSizeMB: Maximum image file size to load for preview and saving.
cfg.imagesThumbnailSize: Choose the thumbnail size used for image attachments in the chat view.
cfg.debugLoggingEnabled: Write diagnostic timing logs to the Codex History Viewer output channel. Logs include counts and durations, not session paths or message content.
